content:
# Sedimentology

**Summary**: The study of the processes and products of lithification.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1995-magaji|Magaji, 1995]] applied sedimentology to characterize the mineralogy and texture of the Wasia sandstone.

- [[1996-matter|Matter, 1996]] applied sedimentology to understand the formation of sedimentary apatite.

- [[2002-siddiqui|Siddiqui, 2002]] analyzed the sedimentary environment of the Unayzah Formation.

- [[1992-al-sulami|Al-Sulami, 1992]] analyzed sedimentology to determine depositional environments.

- [[1988-darwish|Darwish, 1988]] applied sedimentology to interpret the evolution of carbonate systems in the Gulf of Salwa.

- [[2006-al-enezi|Al-Enezi, 2006]] applied sedimentology to characterize the lithofacies of the Dam Formation.

- [[2011-al-masrahy|AL-MASRAHY, 2011]] applied sedimentology to characterize eolian systems in the Unayzah-A.

- [[2011-busubait|Busubait, 2011]] applied sedimentology to characterize the Arab-C facies.

- [[2013-yousif|Yousif, 2013]] used sedimentology to build facies and porosity models for the Hofuf formation.

- [[2014-waseem|Waseem, 2014]] applied sedimentological analysis to characterize the facies and architecture of the Al-Ilb paleochannel.

- [[2014-el-deek|El-Deek, 2014]] applied sedimentology to characterize the glacio-fluvial systems in the Sarah Formation.

- [[2014-mohammedfaiz|Mohammedfaiz, 2014]] applied sedimentology to characterize the Cambro-Ordovician succession.

- [[2015-ali|Ali, 2015]] investigated the sedimentological heterogeneity within the Dam Formation.

- [[2016-hassan-2|Hassan, 2016]] conducted sedimentological analysis to determine lithofacies.

- [[2016-babker|Babker, 2016]] characterized the lithofacies and sedimentological features of the Sarah paleovalley.

- [[2016-bashri|Bashri, 2016]] applied sedimentology to characterize lithofacies in the Dam Formation.

- [[2016-awad|Awad, 2016]] used sedimentology to interpret depositional environments for each formation.

- [[2016-malik|Malik, 2016]] analyzed the sedimentology of the Dhruma Formation.

- [[2016-septriandi|Septriandi, 2016]] integrated foraminiferal biofacies data with established sedimentology to constrain interpretations.

- [[2017-mahgoub|Mahgoub, 2017]] applied sedimentology to describe the depositional environment of the Qusaiba member.

- [[2017-mohammed-2|Mohammed, 2017]] used sedimentological analysis to characterize the Dam Formation.

- [[2017-alqubalee|Alqubalee, 2017]] applied sedimentology to interpret the lithofacies and paleoenvironments of the Sarah Formation.

- [[2017-elyamani|ElYamani, 2017]] applied sedimentology to interpret the depositional history of the Tuwaiq Mountain Formation.

- [[2017-yousif|Yousif, 2017]] used sedimentology to characterize lithofacies and paleoenvironments.

- [[2018-butt|Butt, 2018]] applied sedimentology to characterize the Nutaysh member.

- [[2018-ismanto|Ismanto, 2018]] applied sedimentology to interpret the depositional environment of the Dhruma Formation.

- [[2022-al-alawi|Al-Alawi, 2022]] analyzed the sedimentology of the aeolian system in the Jafurah Sand Sea.

- [[2020-al-zoukani|Al-Zoukani, 2020]] applied sedimentological principles to characterize the D5 and D6 units.

- [[2020-islam|Islam, 2020]] applied sedimentological field investigation to identify lithofacies.

- [[2020-abdallah-2|Abdallah, 2020]] analyzed the thickness of the sedimentary section to determine basin dimensions.

- [[2021-alqarni|Alqarni, 2021]] applied sedimentology to characterize the glacial deposits.

- [[2022-khamsin|Khamsin, 2022]] used sedimentology to interpret the depositional environment of the Dhruma Formation.

- [[2022-khan|Khan, 2022]] conducted sedimentology on the core samples from the King Fahd Causeway.

- [[2022-alshaikhali|Alshaikhali, 2022]] analyzed microplastic concentration within beach sediments.

- [[2022-radwan|Radwan, 2022]] used sedimentological context to interpret provenance and environmental settings.

- [[2022-bashri|Bashri, 2022]] applied sedimentology to characterize the Hanifa Formation.

- [[2022-argadestya|Argadestya, 2022]] analyzed the sedimentary systems from source to sink.

- [[2022-babker|Babker, 2022]] applied sedimentology to interpret the depositional environment of the Khuff Formation.

- [[2023-butt|Butt, 2023]] applied sedimentology to characterize the Al Wajh Formation.

- [[2023-alsahwan|AlSahwan, 2023]] analyzed the sedimentology of sand dunes to understand their physical properties.

- [[2024-hikmahtiar|Hikmahtiar, 2024]] Referenced in this thesis.

- [[2024-alhilali|Alhilali, 2024]] analyzed the sediment context for agglutinated benthic foraminifera.

- [[2024-abdrabo|Abdrabo, 2024]] used sedimentology to identify lithofacies and depositional environments.

- [[2024-malik|Malik, 2024]] analyzed sedimentology to determine the depositional environment.

- [[2024-fahmi|Fahmi, 2024]] used sedimentology to investigate the stratigraphic evolution of the Marrat Formation.

- [[2025-alghawi|Alghawi, 2025]] studied the mineralogical properties of basaltic rocks for carbon storage.

- [[1989-abdou|Abdou, 1989]] used sedimentology to interpret the depositional environment.

- [[1999-babalola|Babalola, 1999]] applied sedimentology to characterize the Wajid Sandstone.

- [[1994-al-guwaizani|Al-Guwaizani, 1994]] applied sedimentology to characterize the facies and mineralogy of the sabkha.

## Methods and Approaches Used

- [[1995-magaji|Magaji, 1995]] ['thin-section-petrography']

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Petrographic analysis revealed a quartz-dominant framework with significant diagenetic alterations. ([[1995-magaji|Magaji, 1995, p. 80]])

- Determined that the deposits formed in a restricted shelf environment. ([[1996-matter|Matter, 1996, p. 26]])

- Identified a complex depositional history involving alluvial fan and playa environments. ([[2002-siddiqui|Siddiqui, 2002, p. 35]])

- Two major shallowing-upward cycles were identified in the Lower Cretaceous sequence. ([[1992-al-sulami|Al-Sulami, 1992, p. 15]])

- The study identified a transition from subtidal lagoonal environments to subaerial facies during the Pleistocene. ([[1988-darwish|Darwish, 1988, p. 108]])

- The Dam Formation is characterized by various carbonate lithofacies including grainstones, packstones, and argillaceous limestones. ([[2006-al-enezi|Al-Enezi, 2006, p. 30]])

- Identified distinct eolian facies including dunes, interdunes, and sand sheets. ([[2011-al-masrahy|AL-MASRAHY, 2011, p. 37]])

- Identified a transition from low energy (tidal flat) to high energy (shoal) environments. ([[2011-busubait|Busubait, 2011, p. 54]])

- Sedimentological analysis identified the Hofuf Formation as representing continental facies with varying porosity (0-40%). ([[2013-yousif|Yousif, 2013, p. 26]])

- Identified distinct lithofacies including trough cross-bedded, horizontally stratified, and ferruginous sandstone. ([[2014-waseem|Waseem, 2014, p. 47]])

- Identified distinct facies types, geometries, and architectural elements across five paleovalleys. ([[2014-el-deek|El-Deek, 2014, p. 15]])

- Identified a variety of sedimentary structures including trough cross-bedding, mud clasts, and syneresis cracks. ([[2014-mohammedfaiz|Mohammedfaiz, 2014, p. 100]])

- Lithofacies such as foraminiferal grainstone show distinct textures like crinkly laminations. ([[2015-ali|Ali, 2015, p. 68]])

- Lithofacies investigation revealed that Dam Formation is composed of mixed carbonate-siliciclastic deposits. ([[2016-hassan-2|Hassan, 2016, p. 13]])

- Identified five distinct lithofacies including diamictite, siltstone, and fluvial sandstone. ([[2016-babker|Babker, 2016, p. 13]])

- Identified diverse sedimentary structures and paleocurrent directions. ([[2016-bashri|Bashri, 2016, p. 41]])

- Identified distinct lithofacies associations (e.g., alluvial fan, fluvial, deltaic). ([[2016-awad|Awad, 2016, p. 17]])

- Ten distinct lithofacies were identified, ranging from oolitic grainstones to mudstones. ([[2016-malik|Malik, 2016, p. 7]])

- The Dam Formation is characterized by sandstone, marl, sandy limestone, clay, conglomerate, and thin gypsum layers. ([[2016-septriandi|Septriandi, 2016, p. 25]])

- The Qusaiba member was deposited in offshore, lower shoreface, middle shoreface, and upper shoreface environments. ([[2017-mahgoub|Mahgoub, 2017, p. 61]])

- Identified 17 lithofacies representing mid-ramp to inner ramp environments. ([[2017-mohammed-2|Mohammed, 2017, p. 16]])

- Identified four distinct lithofacies associations (FA1-FA4) representing different environments. ([[2017-alqubalee|Alqubalee, 2017, p. 65]])

- Identified various carbonate environments including lagoonal settings and high-energy shoals. ([[2017-elyamani|ElYamani, 2017, p. 89]])

- Identified 12 lithofacies in a similar study, grouped into 5-4 associations. ([[2017-yousif|Yousif, 2017, p. 50]])

- Identified four facies associations and thirteen sub-lithofacies in the Nutaysh member. ([[2018-butt|Butt, 2018, p. 2]])

- Dhruma is a good example of an ancient carbonate ramp. ([[2018-ismanto|Ismanto, 2018, p. 83]])

- The system includes dunes, interdunes, sand sheets, and sabkhas influenced by wind and water. ([[2022-al-alawi|Al-Alawi, 2022, p. 60]])

- Sedimentary features like cross-bedding, ripples, and burrowing were used to interpret depositional environments. ([[2020-al-zoukani|Al-Zoukani, 2020, p. 70]])

- Identified two main facies associations: subglacial/diamictite (FA1) and glacial outwash to fluvial channel (FA2). ([[2020-islam|Islam, 2020, p. 54]])

- Sedimentary rocks are typically characterized by low magnetic properties compared to basement rocks. ([[2020-abdallah-2|Abdallah, 2020, p. 22]])

- Identified complex lithofacies associations in the Sarah formation. ([[2021-alqarni|Alqarni, 2021, p. 31]])

- D1A is characterized by green shale with high percentage of Calcium. ([[2022-khamsin|Khamsin, 2022, p. 48]])

- Identified carbonate ramp depositional systems and sedimentary cycles. ([[2022-khan|Khan, 2022, p. 127]])

- Concentrations were measured in both high tide (HTZ) and low tide (LTZ) zones. ([[2022-alshaikhali|Alshaikhali, 2022, p. 49]])

- QCI values in sandstones are linked to eolian depositional environments. ([[2022-radwan|Radwan, 2022, p. 15]])

- Identified nine lithofacies across four facies associations. ([[2022-bashri|Bashri, 2022, p. 19]])

- Identified fluvial, fluvio-coastal-aeolian, and marine influenced deposits. ([[2022-argadestya|Argadestya, 2022, p. 15]])

- Sedimentary facies analysis revealed complex carbonate systems. ([[2022-babker|Babker, 2022]])

- Identified four distinct facies associations (FA1-FA4) representing different depositional environments. ([[2023-butt|Butt, 2023, p. 74]])

- Identified the target material as unconsolidated sedimentary deposits. ([[2023-alsahwan|AlSahwan, 2023, p. 13]])

- Referenced in this thesis. ([[2024-hikmahtiar|Hikmahtiar, 2024]])

- Identified that low sediment overburden (<500 m) ruled out deformation in Ammodiscus peruvianus. ([[2024-alhilali|Alhilali, 2024, p. 98]])

- Identified five distinct lithofacies (LF1-LF5) based on sedimentary features. ([[2024-abdrabo|Abdrabo, 2024, p. 32]])

- Identified 8 lithofacies (F1-F8) and 4 facies associations (FA1-FA4). ([[2024-malik|Malik, 2024, p. 7]])

- Identified five sediment classes: medium carbonate, mud carbonate, sand, silt, and shale. ([[2024-fahmi|Fahmi, 2024, p. 58]])

- Basaltic rock minerals like magnesium and calcium are key to forming carbonates. ([[2025-alghawi|Alghawi, 2025, p. 35]])

- Identified a variety of facies including marly limestones and dolomites. ([[1989-abdou|Abdou, 1989, p. 30]])

- Characterized lithofacies, mineralogy, and sedimentary structures in the Red and Grey units. ([[1999-babalola|Babalola, 1999, p. 12]])

- Sedimentation is influenced by prevailing winds which supply eolian sand to the sabkha. ([[1994-al-guwaizani|Al-Guwaizani, 1994, p. 12]])

## Competing Approaches and Debates

- [[1995-magaji|Magaji, 1995]] Distinguishing primary textures from secondary diagenetic features.
